# @fluenti/react

> React bindings for Fluenti — compile-time i18n with zero runtime overhead.

## Overview

@fluenti/react provides React components, hooks, and a context provider for internationalization. It supports both client-side React and React Server Components (RSC). Built on @fluenti/core, it offers compile-time message extraction with zero runtime overhead for translations.

- Package: @fluenti/react
- Docs: https://fluenti.dev
- Repository: https://github.com/usefluenti/fluenti/tree/main/packages/react
- Peer dependency: react >=18.0.0
- Dependency: @fluenti/core
- License: MIT

## Installation

```bash
pnpm add @fluenti/core @fluenti/react
pnpm add -D @fluenti/cli @fluenti/vite-plugin
```

## Exports — Main Entry (`@fluenti/react`)

The main entry is marked `"use client"` and exports:

### I18nProvider

Context provider that wraps the component tree. Creates a Fluent instance via `createFluent()` from @fluenti/core.

Props (I18nProviderProps):
- locale (string, required): Active locale code
- fallbackLocale (string, optional): Fallback locale when translation is missing
- messages (AllMessages, optional): Static message catalogs keyed by locale
- loadMessages ((locale: string) => Promise<Messages | { default: Messages }>, optional): Async loader for lazy loading locales
- fallbackChain (Record<string, string[]>, optional): Custom fallback chains per locale
- dateFormats (DateFormatOptions, optional): Named date format styles
- numberFormats (NumberFormatOptions, optional): Named number format styles
- missing ((locale: string, id: string) => string | undefined, optional): Missing message handler
- children (ReactNode, required): App content

Behavior:
- Syncs external locale prop changes automatically
- Supports async locale loading with race condition protection
- Exposes i18n instance on `globalThis.__fluenti_i18n` for @fluenti/next webpack loader
- SSR-safe (works with server-side rendering)

### useI18n()

Primary hook for accessing i18n functions. Must be used within `<I18nProvider>`.

Returns (I18nContextValue):
- i18n (FluentInstanceExtended): The underlying Fluent instance with t(), d(), n() methods
  - i18n.t(id, values?): Translate a message
  - i18n.d(value, style?): Format a date
  - i18n.n(value, style?): Format a number
- locale (string): Current locale
- setLocale ((locale: string) => Promise<void>): Change the active locale (async when lazy loading)
- isLoading (boolean): Whether a locale is currently being loaded
- loadedLocales (string[]): Locales whose messages have been loaded
- preloadLocale ((locale: string) => Promise<void>): Preload a locale in the background without switching to it

Throws an error if used outside of `<I18nProvider>`.

### Components

**Trans**: Rich text with JSX component interpolation.
```tsx
<Trans>Read the <a href="/docs">documentation</a></Trans>
```

**Plural**: Locale-aware pluralization via Intl.PluralRules. `#` is replaced with the formatted value.
```tsx
<Plural value={count} one="# item" other="# items" />
```

**Select**: Categorical value selection (gender, role, etc.).
```tsx
<Select value={gender} male="He" female="She" other="They" />
```

**DateTime**: Date formatting via Intl.DateTimeFormat.
```tsx
<p>{i18n.d(new Date(), 'long')}</p>
```

**NumberFormat**: Number formatting via Intl.NumberFormat.
```tsx
<p>{i18n.n(1234.5, 'decimal')}</p>
```

### msg

Lazy message descriptor for use outside the component tree (re-exported from @fluenti/core). Used when auto-injection cannot work.

### I18nContext

Raw React context object for advanced use cases.

### Exported Types

- I18nContextValue
- I18nProviderProps
- TransProps
- PluralProps
- SelectProps
- DateTimeProps
- NumberProps

## Exports — Server Entry (`@fluenti/react/server`)

For React Server Components (Next.js App Router). Uses `React.cache()` for per-request isolation without React Context.

### createServerI18n(config)

Creates request-scoped i18n utilities. Returns a ServerI18n object.

Config (ServerI18nConfig):
- loadMessages ((locale: string) => Promise<Messages | { default: Messages }>, required): Load messages for a locale, called once per locale per request
- fallbackLocale (string, optional): Fallback locale when translation is missing
- resolveLocale ((() => string | Promise<string>), optional): Auto-resolve locale when setLocale() was not called (for Server Actions and other contexts that skip the layout tree)
- fallbackChain (Record<string, Locale[]>, optional): Custom fallback chains
- dateFormats (DateFormatOptions, optional): Named date format styles
- numberFormats (NumberFormatOptions, optional): Named number format styles
- missing ((locale: string, id: string) => string | undefined, optional): Missing key handler

Returns (ServerI18n):
- setLocale(locale: string): void — Set locale for the current request. Call once in root layout.
- getI18n(): Promise<FluentInstanceExtended & { locale: string }> — Get fully configured i18n instance for current request. Messages loaded lazily and cached per-request.
- Trans(props): Promise<ReactElement> — Async server component for rich text. Props: children, id?, comment?, render?
- Plural(props): Promise<ReactElement> — Async server component for pluralization. Props: value, zero?, one?, two?, few?, many?, other, offset?
- DateTime(props): Promise<ReactElement> — Async server component for date formatting. Props: value, style?
- NumberFormat(props): Promise<ReactElement> — Async server component for number formatting. Props: value, style?

### Re-exported SSR Utilities (from @fluenti/core)

- detectLocale: Detect user's preferred locale
- getSSRLocaleScript: Generate script tag for hydrating locale on the client
- getHydratedLocale: Get the hydrated locale value
- isRTL: Check if a locale is right-to-left
- getDirection: Get text direction for a locale

## Full Client-Side Example

```tsx
import { I18nProvider } from '@fluenti/react'
import en from './locales/compiled/en'
import zhCN from './locales/compiled/zh-CN'

function App() {
  return (
    <I18nProvider locale="en" fallbackLocale="en" messages={{ en, 'zh-CN': zhCN }}>
      <MyApp />
    </I18nProvider>
  )
}
```

```tsx
import { useI18n, Trans, Plural, Select } from '@fluenti/react'

function Home() {
  const { i18n, locale, setLocale } = useI18n()

  return (
    <div>
      <h1>{i18n.t('Welcome to my app')}</h1>
      <p>{i18n.t('Hello, {name}!', { name: 'World' })}</p>

      <Trans>Read the <a href="/docs">documentation</a></Trans>

      <Plural value={count} one="# item" other="# items" />

      <Select value={gender} male="He" female="She" other="They" />

      <p>{i18n.d(new Date(), 'long')}</p>
      <p>{i18n.n(1234.5, 'decimal')}</p>

      <button onClick={() => setLocale('en')}>English</button>
      <button onClick={() => setLocale('zh-CN')}>中文</button>
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Full Server Components (RSC) Example

```ts
// lib/i18n.server.ts
import { createServerI18n } from '@fluenti/react/server'

export const { setLocale, getI18n, Trans, Plural, DateTime, NumberFormat } = createServerI18n({
  loadMessages: (locale) => import(`../locales/compiled/${locale}.ts`),
  fallbackLocale: 'en',
  resolveLocale: async () => {
    const { cookies } = await import('next/headers')
    return (await cookies()).get('locale')?.value ?? 'en'
  },
})
```

```tsx
// app/layout.tsx
import { setLocale } from '@/lib/i18n.server'

export default async function Layout({ children }) {
  const locale = /* read from cookie/param */
  setLocale(locale)
  return <html lang={locale}><body>{children}</body></html>
}
```

```tsx
// app/page.tsx — use components directly, no i18n prop needed
import { Trans, Plural, DateTime, NumberFormat } from '@/lib/i18n.server'

export default async function Page() {
  return (
    <div>
      <Trans>Read the <a href="/docs">documentation</a>.</Trans>
      <Plural value={5} one="# item" other="# items" />
      <DateTime value={new Date()} style="long" />
      <NumberFormat value={1234.56} />
    </div>
  )
}
```

## Vite Plugin Setup

```ts
// vite.config.ts
import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
import fluenti from '@fluenti/vite-plugin'

export default {
  plugins: [
    fluenti({ framework: 'react' }),
    react(),
  ],
}
```

## Framework Compatibility

Works with any React-based framework:
- React SPA (Vite)
- React Router
- Remix
- Next.js (App Router / Pages Router)

## Internal Details

- The `__useI18n` hook is an internal variant exported for framework integration
- `globalThis.__fluenti_i18n` is set by I18nProvider for the @fluenti/next webpack loader, which injects `globalThis.__fluenti_i18n.t(...)` into client components to avoid React module boundary issues in Next.js RSC
- The server entry uses `React.cache()` to scope state per-request. It includes a module-level fallback for Server Actions where React.cache() may not share state
- `__getSyncInstance()` is an internal synchronous accessor used by the @fluenti/next webpack loader for t`` in RSC
